original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded Referral Fee Causes Fee Distribution DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The setReferralFee function lacks an upper bound check. If set above 1000 (100%), the fee calculation in GaugeCL will underflow, permanently blocking LP fee distribution.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: dibsPercentage is used to calculate the referral fee: claimed0 * referralFee / 1000. If referralFee > 1000, the calculated fee exceeds the claimed amount. The subsequent subtraction (claimed0 -= _dibsFeeToken0) will revert due to arithmetic underflow.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Missing bounds check on critical fee parameter

operational_questions: Is there a UI validation that prevents setting this > 1000?
What happens to accumulated fees while distribution is blocked?
validation_requirements: Attempt to call distributeFees and verify underflow revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromised or mistaken GAUGE_ADMIN calls setReferralFee(1500)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: GaugeCL._claimFees calculates dibs fee > claimed amount
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Transaction reverts on underflow, blocking all CL gauge fee distributions

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Halt of LP fee distribution to internal bribes
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low, admin can reset the fee to a valid value

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
